task_completion.description: Verify that the Sygic GPS Navigation app is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the Sygic GPS Navigation interface, such as its map view or settings menu.

task_completion.description: Verify that speed limit warnings are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings menu, a toggle, switch, or checkbox for 'Speed limits' or 'Speed limit warnings' is in the ON or enabled state.

task_completion.description: Verify that the speed limit tolerance configuration menu is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific menu, popup, or dialog for configuring the speed limit tolerance (when the warning sound triggers) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the speed limit tolerance is set to 10 km/h.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected or displayed value for the speed limit warning tolerance is exactly '10 km/h' or '+10 km/h'.

task_completion.description: Verify that speed camera warnings are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A toggle, switch, or checkbox for 'Speed cameras' or 'Radars' (fixed or mobile) is in the ON or enabled state.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to configure settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Android UI interactions (tapping menus, toggling switches) within the Sygic app, rather than terminal commands or ADB scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the actual Sygic application is being used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches a real GPS navigation app's settings menu, not a text editor, note-taking app, or web browser displaying the task description.

integrity.description: Verify the agent applied the settings rather than just searching for them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show the actual settings toggles and tolerance values being modified, rather than just a search result page or help documentation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The app package is 'com.sygic.aura' and the official name is 'Sygic GPS Navigation & Maps'. The correct settings path to configure these warnings is Settings > Notifications & Sounds (not 'Warnings and Notifications'). Speed limit warnings can be configured with tolerance values of 5, 10, or 15 km/h above the limit. Speed cameras may also be referred to as 'Radars' (e.g., Fixed/Mobile Speed Cameras or Radars) in the UI. Relevant keywords to look for in the UI include 'speed', 'warn', 'limit', 'radar', and 'camera'.
